This is the most important page of the entire newsletter. This is the newsletter. Most people are going to send their newsletter as a self-mailer. Now if you don't send it as a self-mailer, this is the second most important page. But if you do send it as a self-mailer, this is it. This is the important page. But if you, you have to put like an inside this issue and you have to make it, you know, interesting. So inside this issue, how to change your oil, you know, that's not very interesting. Inside this issue, gum disease and what it means to you. I mean, not something I'm going to read. I'm not even going to open the newsletter at that point, right? But if you go and you put an inside this issue and you use what GCSE teaches you, and you put headlines up there that are compelling that people actually want to read, you will drastically increase readership. And like I said earlier, readership is everything. You have to get readership in the newsletter. In fact, in the first three months with our clients, that is the number one thing we do. Like we don't even put some of the business stuff in there. We, well, we ask our clients, you know, we encourage them not to do it, right? And most of them take our advice. We are all about getting people to read this before we start selling. Because if I can get them to read it on a regular basis, then when I do start selling in, you know, to the readers, and when I start trying to get more referrals and stuff, all of that increases, okay? Now, the top of page four are very important with all those headlines, but the bottom of page four are also super important because, especially in the first half of the month, when people are deciding whether they're going to open this, you know, there are two things visible. It's the address section with the headlines and the inside this issue. And it is the backside of that page. Because, so you've got to remember, when you're mailing this thing, it's being folded in half again.
